title: Apple Sauce
summary: This classic homemade apple sauce is so easy to make, and is much more delicious than the shop-bought stuff. Serve it with roast pork for the perfect Sunday lunch.

method.description: Peel the apples. Coarsely grate the peeled apples into a saucepan.
Add the lemon juice, sugar and butter into the grated apples. Mix to combine then set the pan over a medium-high heat.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then turn the heat down to medium, place a lid on and cook for 15-20 mins, stirring occasionally, until the apples have softened completely.

Once softened, remove the lid. If your apple sauce is too loose for your liking, turn the heat up to medium-high and keep cooking the apple sauce for another 5-10 mins, with the lid off, until thickened.
Taste and season with more sugar, if desired. Serve with roast pork.

chefs.name: Mob
chefs.summary: Mob is on a mission to instil a love of cooking in everyone, because we believe in the positive impact that it can have on people's lives.
